characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: A race of immortal, shape-shiftingmineral-based aliensoriginating from a place only known as the "Gem Homeworld". Achilles' Heel: Destroy or damage their gemstones and they're screwed. Even merely cracking a gemstone greatly increases the likelihood that it will break completely, both because of how easily the crack spreads and theincapacitating effectson their bodies. The Ageless: Gems do not age, and areborn as adults. Alien Fair Folk: They at first appeared to be some sort of magical fae-like race, but were revealed as aliens halfway into the first season. All-Natural Gem Polish: As described inBizarre Alien Reproductionbelow, Gems, such as Amethyst, are sprouted directly from the ground and all so far appear to be worked into unique cuts. Possibly justified by the apparent artificial nature of their creation.

characters.name: Sapphires
characters.description: Rare aristocratic Gems with ice powers and the ability to see into the future. Blue Means Cold: They have ice powers and predominantly blue color schemes. A rarer variety is the Padparadscha Sapphire, who instead has a pink/orange color scheme. Cyclops: The Crystal Gems' Sapphire only has one eye; it can be assumed that the same is true of other Sapphires. Fantastic Honorifics: On Homeworld, the proper way to address a Sapphire is "Your Clarity" or "Your Grand Clarity". It is not known if this is a term specifically for Sapphires or a way to address aristocratic Gems in general. Fire/Ice Duo: They have ice powers to contrast the Rubies and their fire powers. Real rubies and sapphires are both varieties of the same mineral, corundum.

characters.name: Lapis Lazulis
characters.description: Flight: They have the ability to fly using wings made out of water. Hostile Terraforming: Lapis Lazuli's are intended to use their hydrokinetic powers to terraform planets, creating high pressure streams of water that literally slice the land to pieces. Ironic Name: Real lapis lazuli stones dissolve in water. Logical Weakness: They have hydrokinesis, the power to manipulate water. This makes them nigh-unstoppable on water-covered planets like Earth, but utterly powerless on others, like Homeworld. Making a Splash: They can form structures, weapons and golems out of water. These structures can be tall enough to reach space and containallof a planet's oceans.

characters.name: Quartzes
characters.description: Big Fun: Most Quartzes are seen to be jovial and fun-loving. The Ghost: Of the known varieties of Quartz, Citrines are the only ones not shown onscreen. Heroic Build: Quartzes are tall and bulky with a muscular build. Muscles Are Meaningful: Quartzes are typically created to act as soldiers, and most have the muscles to show it. Super-Soldier: The elite soldiers of the Gem empire.

characters.name: Rubies
characters.description: Blood Knight: Since they were made to be soldiers, Rubies are enthusiastic about most things they do, including fighting.Ruby Soldier #1: Hey! Can't wait for those rebels to get here!Ruby Soldier #2: Haha, yeah! When I see those rebels, I'm gonna punch them right in their faces!Ruby Soldier #1: What are you sayin'?I'mgonna punch them all over their bodies, and then it'll be over.Ruby Soldier #2: What if I just punchyou? Expendable Clone: They're not as valued as the Quartz soldiers. The Ditz: Most Rubies aren't that smart and are easily fooled, as shown by the events of "Hit the Diamond". Dumb Muscle: The majority of Rubies don't have much in the brains department. They are, however, tremendously strong. Fire/Ice Duo: They have fire powers to contrast the Sapphires and their ice powers. Real rubies and sapphires are both varieties of the same mineral, corundum.

characters.name: Peridots
characters.description: Green Gems made to serve as engineers and technicians. To compensate for the resource crisis, they were made smaller in Era 2. Cool Shades: All Peridots wear translucent, triangle-shaped visors. Made of Iron: Despite not having much combat capability, "The Kindergarten Kid" shows that Peridots are surprisingly durable and can take a lot of physical punishment without poofing. The Smart Guy: They're mainly used to perform technological work. Shorter Means Smarter: Era 2 Peridots are about the same size as a Ruby or Sapphire, and are given limb enhancers to compensate for their small size.

characters.name: Pearls
characters.description: AServant Racethat works as attendants and handmaidens of higher-ranking Gems. Owning a Pearl is considered a status symbol on Homeworld. Beautiful Slave Girl: Pearls are made for and owned by high-ranking Gems as a status symbol. Peridot describes the main functions of Pearls as standing around, looking nice, and holding stuff. They have also been seen acting as secretaries and courtroom artists. Dainty Little Ballet Dancers: The standard Pearl has a waifish, slender build with a tutu-like outfit and ballet slippers. Gag Nose: All Pearls share the same long, conical nose. Noodle People: This is a common trait amongst all Pearls. They are by far the skinniest Gems in the series.
